content_of_post_to_comment: I don't know what to do anymore. I've tried everything and anything to quit my phone. I used to sit hours and hours a day, like 18, or 16 hours of screen time. And honestly, I've gotten better. I have. But I'm back to relapsing and 8h+ a day of screentime. Grayscale did almost nothing. Every blocker I used was gone by day 2 because I just kept clicking 'ignore'. Uninstalling worked.. for a while. But then I either install it again, genuinely for one thing, and am back in the hole, or I just sit on another device. I've tried activity jars. Picking up new hobbies. Convincing myself I don't want to sit on my phone.. because I really don't. And the only thing that works is discipline and leaving it behind and going out, which is a pain in the ass because no music, no contact, no navigation. And I don't always have discipline. I have worse days like yesterday where it's gone, out the window, and I end up back to 12 hours of screen time, on games and mindless scrolling. Can anyone recommend me anything, even unhinged stuff, to force myself to be more productive? And things to do when not productive instead of my phone.
comment: The part about blockers failing because you just hit ignore is something a lot of people miss. Sometimes the goal can't be making the phone impossible to access, but making the default option less automatic. I’ve found it helps to add friction before scrolling, like keeping the phone in another room and deciding specific times to check it. The boredom that shows up after that is uncomfortable, but it’s also where your brain starts adjusting.
